title: cordylanthus rigidus subsp. brevibracteatus
intro: Cordylanthus rigidus subsp. brevibracteatus, also known as the short-bracted bird's beak, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family, Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of iridoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis. Cordylanthus rigidus subsp. brevibracteatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal compounds to be utilized effectively.
